claimText: The 10 Non-Permanent Members of the UN Security Council are elected by the UN General Assembly for a two-year term through a secret ballot.
evidenceExcerpt: 10 Non-Permanent Members, elected by UNGA for a two-year term through secret ballot.
examUse: Statement-based question on the election process and tenure of UNSC non-permanent members.
distractorHooks: Elected by the permanent members of the UNSC
Elected for a three-year term
Elected through an open voting system

claimText: In the regional distribution of UNSC non-permanent seats, Africa holds the highest number of seats with three.
evidenceExcerpt: Regional Distribution: Africa (3), Asia (2), Latin America (2), Western Europe and others (2) & Eastern Europe (1).
examUse: Fact-based statement or comparison question on the regional representation in the UNSC.
distractorHooks: Asia holds the highest number of non-permanent seats
Africa and Asia hold an equal number of seats
Latin America holds three seats

claimText: The UN Security Council has the authority to make binding decisions that member states are obligated to implement under the UN Charter.
evidenceExcerpt: UNSC has the power to make binding decisions that member states must implement under the UN Charter.
examUse: Statement-based question comparing the binding nature of UNSC resolutions versus the recommendatory nature of UNGA resolutions.
distractorHooks: UNSC decisions are only advisory in nature
Member states can opt out of implementing UNSC decisions
UNSC decisions require UNGA approval to become binding

claimText: India has been elected to a non-permanent seat on the UN Security Council eight times, most recently for the 2021-2022 term.
evidenceExcerpt: India has been elected to the non-permanent seat of the UNSC eight times, with the most recent term being 2021-2022.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's historical engagement and representation in the UN Security Council.
distractorHooks: India has been elected to the UNSC ten times
India has never served as a non-permanent member of the UNSC
India's most recent term was in 2011-2012

claimText: The G4 Nations, comprising India, Brazil, Germany, and Japan, advocate for UN Security Council reform.
evidenceExcerpt: UNSC Reform Proposal (2024): Proposed by G4 Nations (India, Brazil, Germany, and Japan).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching international groupings to their member states or primary objectives.
distractorHooks: The G4 includes South Africa
The G4 opposes the expansion of the UNSC
The G4 is an economic bloc focused on free trade

claimText: The C10 (Committee of Ten) represents the African Union's Common Position on UN Security Council reform.
evidenceExcerpt: C10 (Committee of Ten): 10 African Union states... represents Africa's Common Position on UNSC reform.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching the C10 group to its objective regarding UN reforms.
distractorHooks: C10 represents the top 10 financial contributors to the UN budget
C10 is a coalition of European nations seeking UNSC reform
C10 opposes any changes to the current UNSC structure

claimText: The 'Uniting for Consensus' coalition, also known as the 'Coffee Club', opposes the creation of new permanent seats in the UN Security Council.
evidenceExcerpt: The 'Uniting for Consensus/Coffee Club' coalition opposes the creation of new permanent seats.
examUse: Statement-based question on the stances of various international coalitions regarding UNSC expansion.
distractorHooks: The Coffee Club supports adding new permanent members without veto power
Uniting for Consensus advocates for the abolition of the UNSC
The Coffee Club is led by the G4 nations

claimText: Amending the UN Charter to reform the UNSC requires a two-thirds vote in the General Assembly and ratification by all five permanent members.
evidenceExcerpt: Requires a UN Charter amendment, needing a two-thirds General Assembly vote and ratification by all five permanent members.
examUse: Statement-based question on the procedural and structural barriers to amending the UN Charter.
distractorHooks: Requires a simple majority vote in the UN General Assembly
Requires ratification by only three of the five permanent members
Can be amended by the UNGA without the approval of the permanent members
